name: Paloma

spec.method_detail: Salt rim optional. Build in ice-filled highball. Add tequila and lime. Top with grapefruit soda. Stir gently.

history: The Paloma (Spanish for 'dove') is arguably more popular in Mexico than the Margarita. The origin is murky — possibly created by Don Javier Delgado Corona at La Capilla in Tequila, Jalisco. The traditional Mexican version uses Squirt or Jarritos grapefruit soda; craft versions use fresh grapefruit juice and club soda. Either way it's refreshing, low-alcohol, and underrated in the U.S.
